sentenceFrame: The light is high in the night.
celebration: Two spellings for long i — igh (night, light, high) and ie (pie, tie). igh is three letters holding one long-i sound, silent g and h. ie is a tiny family — pie, tie, die, lie — and the class meets all of it in one week. Right-branch leaf #4.

songs.title: igh-igh-Night!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(eye… eye… eye…)
Eye! Eye! Night!

[Hook]
I-G-H — three letters! (one sound!)
Silent G, silent H! (shhh!)
igh-igh-night! igh-igh-night!
The I says its name! (eye!)

[Verse 2]
N-I-G-H-T — night! (in the night!)
L-I-G-H-T — light! (a little light!)
igh-igh-night! igh-igh-night!
Up so high! (way up high!)

[Verse 3]
P-I-E — pie! (a big pie!)
T-I-E — tie! (a bow tie!)
igh-igh-night! igh-igh-night!
The light is high in the night!

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! No igh!
(eye… eye… eye…)

[Outro]
Night… light… high… (shhh…)
Eye! Eye! Night!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: igh and ie both sing long-i /aɪ/ (phonetic respelling: sung 'eye', printed igh/ie). The lesson: igh is THREE letters, one sound, with a silent g and h ('shhh'). ie is the small end-of-word family (pie, tie). Spell-outs uppercase. A night song — keep it hushed and glowing, the whisper-rap verses suit it. Potato has no igh.

songs.title: The Light at Night
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
The light is high in the night!
The light is high in the night!
High, high! (way up high!)
The light is high in the night!

[Verse 2]
Sheep can see the light!
Bee can see the light!
High, high! (way up high!)
A light in the night!

[Verse 3]
A pie! A pie! (up so high!)
The pie is on the light?! (way up there!)
High, high! (way up high!)
Bee can see the pie!

[Outro]
The light… the night… (shhh…)
In the night?! (a potato!)
(POTATO!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'The light is high in the night.' — a gentle nighttime song, glowing and calm. 'see' carries the ee/ea team from last week. Whisper the outro; potato hits loud, glowing like a nightlight.

spreads.artNote: A deep blue-black night sky scattered with stars, the dark forest hushed below. A new mood for Level 3.
A single warm lantern glowing in the dark, the first light in the night.
The lantern lifted high on a pole, its glow spreading over the trees. Reaching upward.

spreads.text: The light is in the night.
spreads.artNote: The whole dark scene warmed by the one high light — stars above, a soft golden pool below. Peaceful.
Sheep — fluffy round white sheep with kind dark eyes — gazing up at the light, wool tinged gold, calm and safe.
A golden pie with a lattice crust glowing in the lantern light — someone left a pie out at night.
The pie perched improbably on a very high shelf, just out of reach. Comic longing.
Bee — plump gold-and-black honeybee with round shiny wings — hovering right up at the high pie, delighted, having flown to it easily. Bee wins.
On the high shelf beside where the pie was, a single potato glows softly like a little nightlight. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it. Sheep and Bee stare up in wonder.

sentences: The light is high in the night.
extras: igh — THREE LETTERS, ONE SOUND: the i says its name /aɪ/ and the g and h are silent partners holding hands (night, light, high, right, bright). Trace all three letters as one gesture, whisper nothing at gh.
ie — THE SMALL FAMILY: ie says long i at the end of a short word. There are only four: pie, tie, die, lie. Meet all of them at once and be done.
DO NOT introduce 'knight' — its silent k arrives at Week 56. If it comes up, park it warmly.
Right-branch leaf #4 goes up: read night/light/high/pie aloud, hang the igh/ie leaf.
Moveable alphabet: build n-igh-t, l-igh-t, h-igh, p-ie, t-ie — weld igh as one three-letter piece.

teacherFocus: LONG I, TWO SPELLINGS. igh is three letters holding one long-i sound — the i says its name, the g and h are silent (night, light, high, right, bright). Trace all three as one gesture. ie is the small end-of-word family — only four words: pie, tie, die, lie — meet them all and move on. Do NOT introduce 'knight'; its silent k belongs to Week 56. Hang right-branch leaf #4.
